attractions.name: Alley Bar
attractions.description: Alley Bar is an Ann Arbor institution with a history of serving drinks at its location for over 130 years, dating back to Charles Binder's Saloon in 1880. The bar aims to bridge the gap between a relaxed cocktail bar and an upscale dive, offering quality fresh ingredients, Michigan craft beers, and simple, relaxed vibes. Guests can enjoy their drinks in historic 60-year-old booths salvaged from the old Round Table Tavern.
highlights: Historical location operating since 1880, originally Charles Binder's Saloon.
Weekly events including Martini Mondays and Beer Can Tuesdays with Darts Tournaments.
Wayback Wednesdays featuring DJ Tres Styles and throwback jams starting at 9pm.
Weekend Happy Hour on Fridays and Saturdays from 5pm-8pm with 50% off draft beers.
Unique interior featuring 60-year-old booths from the former Round Table Tavern.
Extensive cocktail menu featuring house-made ingredients and Michigan spirits.

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Art Center (A2AC)
attractions.description: Established in 1909, the Ann Arbor Art Center (A2AC) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to sparking creativity through provocative exhibitions, accessible art classes, and a shop featuring regional artists. It serves as a vital community hub for visual arts in downtown Ann Arbor, offering programming for all ages and skill levels.
attractions.address: 117 W. Liberty St., Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: Rotating contemporary art exhibitions in the street-level A2AC Gallery
Year-round art classes and workshops in ceramics, jewelry, painting, and digital media
A2AC Shop featuring unique handmade works from regional Michigan artists
Community-focused events such as Artini and free family drop-in workshops
Specialized Art Camps and educational programs for kids and teens
Public art initiatives including the A2AC Murals in downtown Ann Arbor

attractions.name: Ann Arbor District Library (Downtown Library)
attractions.description: The Ann Arbor District Library (AADL) is a premier public library system serving the residents of the Ann Arbor school district. The Downtown Library, a centerpiece of the community since 1957, offers an extensive collection of materials, technology resources, and diverse public programming in a modern, multi-story facility. Wikipedia, AADL
highlights: Extensive Local History section with city directories and genealogy resources AADL
Youth area featuring a saltwater fish tank and a hidden 'fairy door' AADL
Public computer terminals, Mac training lab, and assistive technology for the visually impaired AADL
Rotating art exhibits and permanent installations throughout the building AADL
Large circulating collection of over 480,000 items including art prints and tools Wikipedia
Free Wi-Fi and self-bookable meeting rooms for community use AADL

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Farmers Market
attractions.description: The Ann Arbor Farmers Market is a historic, producer-only market that has served the community for over 100 years. It features 130 vendors from Michigan offering fresh produce, artisan crafts, prepared foods, and seasonal items in an open-air pavilion. (City of Ann Arbor)
attractions.days_open: Saturdays (Year-round); Wednesdays (May - December) (City of Ann Arbor)
highlights: Over 130 local Michigan vendors (City of Ann Arbor)
Producer-only market policy (City of Ann Arbor)
Monthly food truck rallies from July to October (City of Ann Arbor)
Seasonal cooking demonstrations and kids' programming (City of Ann Arbor)
Wide selection of artisan goods including jewelry, ceramics, and textiles (City of Ann Arbor)
Accepts SNAP/EBT and Double Up Food Bucks (City of Ann Arbor)

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Film Festival (AAFF)
attractions.description: The Ann Arbor Film Festival (AAFF) is the oldest avant-garde and experimental film festival in North America and an annual Oscar-qualifying event. Founded in 1963, it serves as a premiere forum for independent filmmakers, showcasing a diverse range of experimental, animation, documentary, and fiction films from around the globe (AAFF History, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 603 E Liberty St, Ann Arbor, MI 48104 (Event Location: Michigan Theater)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday - Sunday (during the annual festival week in March) (AAFF 64 Page)
highlights: Oldest experimental film festival in North America, founded in 1963 (Wikipedia)
Oscar-qualifying festival for short films in the competition categories (AAFF FAQ)
Presents 40 programs featuring over 180 films from more than 20 countries (AAFF History)
Features "Off the Screen" installations, performances, and new media works (AAFF 64 Page)
Nightly after-parties held at local downtown venues like Knight's on Liberty (AAFF Blog)
Historically premiered early works by legends like George Lucas, Andy Warhol, and Yoko Ono (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Folk Festival
attractions.description: The Ann Arbor Folk Festival is a two-night extravaganza showcasing a blend of renowned headliners and up-and-coming folk, roots, and acoustic artists. As the largest annual fundraiser for The Ark, the event supports Ann Arbor's non-profit home for live music and has been a local tradition since 1976 (The Ark).
attractions.days_open: Friday and Saturday (Annual event in late January) (The Ark)
highlights: Headlining performances by major artists like Greensky Bluegrass and Amos Lee
Discovery of emerging talent through the 'Find Your Folk' showcases
Annual fundraiser benefiting The Ark, a legendary non-profit music venue
Held at the historic and acoustically renowned Hill Auditorium
Diverse lineup spanning bluegrass, roots, rock, and contemporary folk genres
Pre-Glow Party and special perks for Platinum and Benefactor ticket holders

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Hands-On Museum
attractions.description: The Ann Arbor Hands-On Museum is a premier interactive science center located in a historic firehouse in downtown Ann Arbor. It features over 250 exhibits across several floors, designed to inspire curiosity and discovery in science, technology, engineering, art, and math (STEAM) for visitors of all ages. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 220 E. Ann St, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: Over 250 interactive exhibits spread across 10 distinct gallery areas. (Official Website)
Housed in the historic 1882 Ann Arbor Central Fire Station, a landmark on the National Register of Historic Places. (Wikipedia)
The Preschool Gallery, a dedicated space for children aged 5 and under to explore and play safely. (Official Website)
The World Around You gallery, featuring a popular water play area and a giant bubble capsule. (Metro Parent, Inventors of Tomorrow)
The Block Party exhibit, where visitors can use thousands of foam blocks to build and design structures. (Metro Parent)
Critters Up Close, a monthly live wildlife series held on the last Saturday and Sunday of each month. (Official Website)

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Hash Bash
attractions.description: Hash Bash is an annual cannabis activism event and celebration held in Ann Arbor, Michigan, featuring speeches, live music, and advocacy for marijuana law reform. Originating in 1972, the event traditionally takes place at 'high noon' on the University of Michigan Diag on the first Saturday of April.
attractions.address: University of Michigan Diag (Intersection of State St. and N. University Ave.)
highlights: 'High Noon' rally on the University of Michigan Diag with guest speakers and activists.
Hash Bash Cup featuring vendors, celebrity guests, glassblowers, and live performances.
Historical significance as one of the longest-running cannabis reform events in the U.S.
Gathering of cannabis enthusiasts and advocates from across the country.
Fools Freak Fest at the Blind Pig featuring local musical acts.
Annual awards show recognizing leaders in the cannabis industry and community.

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Restaurant Week
attractions.description: Ann Arbor Restaurant Week is a premier culinary event that showcases the diverse dining scene of downtown Ann Arbor. Featuring over 40 participating restaurants, the week-long celebration offers diners the chance to experience prix-fixe menus, special deals, and unique tastings. It is designed to highlight the talent of local chefs and encourage the community to explore new flavors and revisit neighborhood favorites. Destination Ann Arbor
attractions.address: Various locations in Downtown Ann Arbor (Organizer office: 15 Research Dr)
attractions.days_open: Sunday, January 18 - Friday, January 23, 2026 Ann Arbor Restaurant Week
highlights: Prix-fixe multi-course lunch and dinner menus at over 40 participating locations. Ann Arbor Restaurant Week
Diverse menu options including vegetarian, vegan, and family meals to-go. Ann Arbor Restaurant Week
Exclusive "Menu for Two" deals and "Chef\

attractions.name: Ann Arbor Summer Festival (A2SF)
attractions.description: The Ann Arbor Summer Festival (A2SF) is an annual community celebration that champions performing arts, outdoor entertainment, and local spirit. It features a diverse lineup of free and ticketed events, including live music, outdoor film screenings, contemporary circus, and wellness activities. Primarily centered at Top of the Park on the University of Michigan campus, the festival attracts over 80,000 visitors each season Destination Ann Arbor, A2SF History.
attractions.address: 915 E. Washington Street (Top of the Park)
highlights: KidZone: Hands-on activities and creative experiences for children and families.
Movies by Moonlight: Free outdoor film screenings under the stars at Top of the Park.
Retreat Series: Daily wellness activities including yoga, pilates, and dance workshops.
Culinary Row: A curated selection of local food vendors and beverage stands.
The Annex: A dedicated space for interactive workshops, comedy, and intimate performances.
Live Music: A mix of local talent and world-class headliners across multiple stages.

attractions.name: Arbor Brewing Company Session Room
attractions.description: Arbor Brewing Company Session Room is a premier taproom and beer garden located on Ann Arbor's west side, housed in a historic brick warehouse. It features a scratch-made menu with a local and organic focus, complemented by 70 craft beers on tap, including Arbor Brewing Company's own brews and selections from around the world.
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
highlights: 70 craft beers on tap featuring local and international selections
Spacious premier outdoor beer garden
Housed in a historic brick warehouse on the West Side
Scratch-made menu with local and organic ingredients
Full service restaurant with indoor dining and private event options
Online ordering and gift cards available

attractions.name: Ashley's Restaurant and Pub
attractions.description: Since 1983, Ashley's has been a downtown Ann Arbor institution and a leader in the craft beer movement, offering an extensive selection of over 1,000 rotating beers annually and a premier whiskey bar. The menu features scratch-made American gastropub plates, including their signature smash burgers and house-made spent grain bread, served in a lively atmosphere just steps from the University of Michigan campus ashleys.com, Tripadvisor.
highlights: World-class beer selection with over 1,000 different beers featured annually ashleys.com
Extensive whiskey bar program featuring small-batch Bourbons and Scotch single malts ashleys.com
Scratch-made American pub classics like the signature Smash Burger and Pesto Fries ashleys.com
Centrally located in the State Street District, adjacent to the University of Michigan campus annarbor.org
Lively community hub hosting regular trivia nights and popular game day celebrations ashleys.com
"Friend of the Owner" loyalty program rewarding frequent visitors with exclusive offers ashleys.com

attractions.name: Aut Bar (\aut\ BAR)
attractions.description: Aut Bar was a cornerstone of Ann Arbor's LGBTQ+ community for 25 years, serving as the city's first full-time gay-owned gay bar. Located in the historic Braun Court, it was celebrated for its inclusive atmosphere, vibrant outdoor courtyard, and legendary weekend brunches. Although the physical location closed in 2020, its legacy continues through community reunions and its status as a landmark of queer history in Michigan.
highlights: First full-time gay-owned and operated LGBTQ+ bar in Ann Arbor, established in 1995.
Famous weekend brunch featuring signature Eggs Benedict and bottomless mimosas.
Atmospheric outdoor courtyard in Braun Court, decorated with rainbow fairy lights and used for community vigils and celebrations.
A long-standing hub for political activism, including high-profile legal challenges for First Amendment rights.
Home to the popular 'Trivia with Terry' nights and various drag entertainment.
Known for its 'straight-friendly' philosophy, fostering a diverse and welcoming environment for all patrons.

attractions.name: Aventura
attractions.description: Aventura is a vibrant Spanish restaurant located in the heart of downtown Ann Arbor, offering an authentic tapas and paella experience. Housed in a historic 1872 building, the space features captivating rustic brick arches and original stonework, creating a "soul-transporting" atmosphere for social dining Aventura.
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
highlights: Traditional Spanish tapas and world-renowned paella
Extensive list of Spanish wines and craft cocktails
Historic setting in an 1800s building with original masonry
Daily Happy Hour from 3:00 PM to 6:00 PM at the bar
"Sunday Like a Spaniard" special featuring 25% off paellas
Summer Paella Master Classes with expert chefs

attractions.name: Bandemer Park
attractions.description: Bandemer Park is a 38-acre natural area and recreational park situated along the western banks of the Huron River and Argo Pond. It features a diverse range of landscapes, including dry prairies and wet meadows, and serves as a vital segment of the Border-to-Border (B2B) Trail. (City of Ann Arbor)
attractions.address: 2001 Whitmore Lake Road, Ann Arbor, MI 48105 (North Entrance); 1300 Lake Shore Drive, Ann Arbor, MI 48104 (South Entrance)
highlights: Huron River access for rowing and fishing (City of Ann Arbor)
9-hole disc golf course with concrete tees (UDisc)
Dirt bike jump course for BMX and mountain biking (City of Ann Arbor)
Part of the Border-to-Border (B2B) non-motorized trail (B2B Trail)
Accessible canoe dock and seasonal boat lockers (City of Ann Arbor)
Picnic shelter and fire circle available for rent (City of Ann Arbor)

attractions.name: Belle Isle Park
attractions.description: Belle Isle Park is a historic 985-acre island park situated in the Detroit River between the United States and Canada. Designed by Frederick Law Olmsted, it features iconic attractions such as the Belle Isle Aquarium, the Anna Scripps Whitcomb Conservatory, and spectacular views of the Detroit and Windsor skylines.
highlights: Belle Isle Aquarium (oldest original aquarium in the U.S.)
Anna Scripps Whitcomb Conservatory and botanical gardens
James Scott Memorial Fountain
Dossin Great Lakes Museum
Belle Isle Nature Center
Public swimming beach and Iron Belle Trail trailhead

attractions.name: Bird Hills Nature Area
attractions.description: Bird Hills Nature Area is the largest natural area in Ann Arbor, spanning 146 acres of hilly, unpaved woodland trails. It serves as a sanctuary for hikers with its diverse ecosystem of beech, sugar maple, and oak-hickory forests, and is a prime spot for birdwatching and observing rare plant species. City of Ann Arbor, Ann Arbor Observer
attractions.days_open: Daily, year-round City of Ann Arbor
highlights: Largest city park in Ann Arbor at 146 acres City of Ann Arbor
Extensive network of unpaved, hilly hiking trails Ann Arbor Observer
Ranks second in the city for Floristic Quality Assessment with over 350 plant species Defenders.org Stewardship Plan
Confirmed nesting site for turkey vultures Defenders.org Stewardship Plan
Historical remnants including old concrete wagon tracks from its agricultural past City of Ann Arbor
Direct trail connections to Barton and Kuebler Langford Nature Areas City of Ann Arbor

attractions.name: Bløm Mead + Cider (Bløm Meadworks)
attractions.description: Bløm Mead + Cider (pronounced “bloom”) is a meadery and taproom that specializes in "session" meads and ciders, which are light, dry, and carbonated. Using 100% Michigan-grown ingredients, they offer a rotating seasonal menu of beverages that are naturally gluten-free. The downtown Ann Arbor space is designed for community engagement, featuring board games, film screenings, and a variety of weekly events.
attractions.address: 100 S 4th Ave Suite 110, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: Handcrafted session-style meads and ciders made with 100% Michigan ingredients.
Naturally gluten-free beverage options including meads, ciders, and non-alcoholic drinks.
Community-focused taproom with a large collection of board games and a projector for events.
Weekly programmed events such as Live Trivia, Board Game Nights, and "Babies & Brews."
A rotating "nosh" menu featuring seasonal snacks and sweet treats from local producers.
Sustainability-driven business model supporting local farmers and eco-friendly practices.

attractions.name: Border-to-Border Trail (B2B Trail)
attractions.description: The Border-to-Border Trail is a 55-mile non-motorized, paved pathway that connects cities, parks, and nature across Washtenaw County, Michigan. It primarily follows the Huron River and is designed for activities such as walking, running, biking, and rollerblading. B2B Trail
highlights: Scenic riverfront views along the Huron River B2B Trail
ADA-compliant paved pathways for all-ages accessibility B2B Trail
Connections to major regional hubs including Ann Arbor, Chelsea, and Ypsilanti Wikipedia
Integration with the statewide Iron Belle Trail network Wikipedia
Direct access to two canoe and kayak liveries in Ann Arbor B2B Trail

attractions.name: Buhr Park
attractions.description: Buhr Park is a sprawling 39-acre recreation destination in southeastern Ann Arbor featuring a wide array of seasonal and year-round amenities. It is home to a popular outdoor pool and ice arena, historic Cobblestone Farm, and unique environmental features like the Children's Wet Meadow. (Sources: City of Ann Arbor, Wikipedia)
highlights: Outdoor swimming pool and children's wading pool with interactive toys
Refrigerated outdoor ice arena (covered) for skating and hockey
Historic Cobblestone Farm and Museum located on-site
Buhr Park Children's Wet Meadow educational ecosystem
Six tennis courts, softball diamonds, and a Gaga Ball pit
Sledding hill and groomed cross-country ski trails in winter

attractions.name: Burns Park (City of Ann Arbor)
attractions.description: Burns Park is a historic 15-acre neighborhood park in Ann Arbor, Michigan, known for its lush perimeter of shady trees that follow the course of an old horse race track. It offers a wide range of recreational facilities, including sports fields, tennis and pickleball courts, and a popular playground, serving as a central hub for community activity. (City of Ann Arbor)
attractions.address: 1300 Baldwin Ave., Ann Arbor, MI 48104 (City of Ann Arbor)
highlights: Large 15-acre green space with historic horse track perimeter (City of Ann Arbor)
Multiple sports facilities including tennis, pickleball, and basketball courts (City of Ann Arbor)
Extensive playground and seasonal sledding hill (City of Ann Arbor, Ann Arbor Observer)
Home to the Ann Arbor Senior Center and adjacent to Burns Park Elementary (City of Ann Arbor)
Unique petanque court and two environmentally friendly rain gardens (City of Ann Arbor)
Historic Warming Hut located on the site of an 1800s log cabin (City of Ann Arbor)

attractions.name: Burton Memorial Tower
attractions.description: The Burton Memorial Tower is an iconic Art Deco landmark at the University of Michigan, housing the 53-bell Charles Baird Carillon. Completed in 1936 and designed by Albert Kahn, it serves as a memorial to University President Marion Leroy Burton and contains classrooms and offices for the Department of Musicology. SMTD, Wikipedia
highlights: Houses the 53-bell Charles Baird Carillon, one of the heaviest in the world Wikipedia
Designed by renowned architect Albert Kahn in Art Deco and Art Moderne styles SMTD
Features daily carillon recitals at noon during the academic year SMTD
Equipped with a programmable LED illumination system in the belfry SMTD
Stands as a prominent 192-foot vertical landmark on the university's Central Campus Wikipedia

attractions.name: Chelsea Alehouse Brewery (Pure Michigan)
attractions.description: Chelsea Alehouse Brewery was a beloved local craft brewery and pub that operated for eleven years in downtown Chelsea, Michigan. It featured a scratch kitchen serving sandwiches and salads alongside beers brewed on-site, as well as a selection of Michigan-made brews, wine, and craft cocktails (Ann Arbor Observer, Fox 2 Detroit).
attractions.address: 115 S Main St, Chelsea, MI 48118 (Pure Michigan)
attractions.days_open: Closed in May 2024 (Ann Arbor Observer); previously open Wednesday through Sunday (Oak Rowan Foodie)
highlights: Beers brewed on-site in a 7-barrel brewery (Drink Michigan)
Live music events including bluegrass, rock, folk, and jazz (Yelp, Life In Michigan)
Scratch kitchen featuring locally sourced ingredients and house-made salsa (Yelp, Life In Michigan)
Inviting atmosphere with exposed brick walls and hardwood floors (Oak Rowan Foodie)
Family-friendly environment with darts, billiards, and board games (Yelp)

attractions.name: Chelsea Milling Company ("JIFFY" Mix)
attractions.description: Chelsea Milling Company, a family-owned business since 1901, is the historic manufacturer of "JIFFY" Mix baking products. The company is renowned for creating the first prepared baking mix in the United States and continues to operate out of its iconic facility in downtown Chelsea. While in-person factory tours have been discontinued, the site remains a major local landmark famous for its massive grain elevators (Jiffy Mix Official Site, Wikipedia).
attractions.days_open: Monday – Friday (Business Hours) (Jiffy Mix Official Site)
highlights: Features iconic grain elevators displaying the recognizable "JIFFY" box logo (Wikipedia)
The birthplace of the first prepared baking mix in the U.S., developed in 1930 (Wikipedia)
Family-owned and operated by the Holmes family for over 120 years (Jiffy Mix Official Site)
Produces approximately 1.6 million boxes of product daily during peak season (Tripadvisor)
Offers a detailed virtual tour experience showcasing the manufacturing process (Jiffy Mix Official Site)
Maintains a strong commitment to using locally sourced, American-grown ingredients (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Cobblestone Farm and Museum
attractions.description: Cobblestone Farm is a living history museum and community event center anchored by a rare 1844 cobblestone farmhouse and a three-story timber frame barn. The site provides a glimpse into 19th-century rural life in Washtenaw County and serves as a unique venue for weddings, festivals, and educational programs. City of Ann Arbor, Destination Ann Arbor
attractions.days_open: Tuesday (Drop-in tours), Monday-Friday (Office hours), 3rd Sunday of each month (Farmhouse museum) City of Ann Arbor
highlights: Guided tours of the 1844 cobblestone farmhouse restored to its mid-19th-century appearance. City of Ann Arbor
Historic three-story timber frame barn used for weddings and community events. City of Ann Arbor
Live demonstrations including cooking on a wood stove and loom weaving during annual festivals. Cobblestone Farm Association
A barnyard featuring farm animals like goats, geese, and chickens. Tripadvisor
Annual community events such as the Harvest Festival, Independence Day celebration, and Country Christmas. Cobblestone Farm Association

attractions.name: Depot Town Historic District
attractions.description: Depot Town is a historic commercial district in Ypsilanti, Michigan, located along East Cross and River Streets near the Huron River (Wikipedia). Established in the 1830s around the city's train depot, the area is renowned for its beautifully restored 19th-century architecture and vibrant community spirit (City of Ypsilanti). Today, it serves as a hub for independent shops, antique dealers, and popular local restaurants while hosting major annual festivals (Ypsilanti DDA).
attractions.address: E Cross St & N River St, Ypsilanti, MI 48198
highlights: Ypsilanti Automotive Heritage Museum, featuring the last Hudson dealership (Wikipedia)
Sidetrack Bar & Grill, operating continuously since 1850 (Ypsilanti DDA)
Historic Ypsilanti Freighthouse, a center for community events and farmers' markets (Wikipedia)
Riverside and Frog Island Parks, connected by the iconic 'Tridge' footbridge (Destination Ann Arbor)
Major festivals including the Michigan Brewers Guild Summer Beer Festival and Michigan ElvisFest (Wikipedia)
The Thompson Block, a historic building that served as Civil War barracks (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Detroit Historical Museum
attractions.description: The Detroit Historical Museum chronicles the life and times of the Detroit region, safeguarding its rich history through immersive experiences. It features iconic exhibits that showcase Detroit's transformation from a rural frontier town to the industrial 'Motor City' and a global cultural leader. Detroit Historical Society
highlights: Streets of Old Detroit: A signature exhibit recreating 19th-century street scenes. Detroit Historical Society
America’s Motor City: Explores how cars built metro Detroit and the city's role as an industrial powerhouse. Detroit Historical Society
The Glancy Trains: A massive, detailed toy train collection and a visitor favorite. Detroit Historical Society
Legends Plaza: An outdoor area honoring Detroit's cultural icons with handprints and signatures. Detroit Historical Society
Motor City Music: An interactive space celebrating the city's rich musical legacy. Detroit Historical Society

attractions.name: Detroit Institute of Arts Official Site
attractions.description: The Detroit Institute of Arts (DIA) features one of the largest and most significant art collections in the United States, with over 65,000 works spanning from ancient times to the present. Housed in a magnificent Beaux-Arts building in Detroit's Midtown, it is renowned for its encyclopedic collection and the iconic "Detroit Industry Murals" by Diego Rivera. The museum serves as a major cultural hub, offering diverse exhibitions, programs, and a world-class center for African American art. Wikipedia, Official Site
highlights: Diego Rivera\
Detroit Industry Murals
s "Self-Portrait" (1887) Official Site
Pieter Bruegel the Elder\
The Wedding Dance
s "The Thinker" (cast at entrance) Official Site
Kresge Court historic courtyard and dining area Official Site
Center for African American Art Official Site

attractions.name: Detroit International Riverfront (also known as the Detroit Riverwalk) (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy)
attractions.description: The Detroit International Riverfront is a scenic 5.5-mile promenade stretching along the Detroit River from the Ambassador Bridge to Belle Isle. It features a diverse collection of parks, plazas, and greenways that offer recreational opportunities and stunning views of the city skyline and Canada. This landmark has been recognized as the best riverwalk in the United States for three consecutive years by USA Today (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 1340 Atwater St (Cullen Plaza) or 600 Renaissance Center, Suite 1720
highlights: Cullen Family Carousel featuring creatures indigenous to the Detroit River (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy)
Dequindre Cut Greenway, a non-motorized trail connecting the riverfront to Eastern Market (Wikipedia)
William G. Milliken State Park and Harbor, Michigan's first urban state park (Visit Detroit)
Mt. Elliott Splash Pad and family-friendly interactive water features (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy)
Robert C. Valade Park with its sandy beach, playground, and seasonal sledding hill (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy)
Aretha Franklin Amphitheater, a premier waterfront venue for jazz and soul concerts (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Detroit Zoo
attractions.description: The Detroit Zoo is a world-renowned wildlife destination spanning 125 acres and housing more than 2,000 animals across 200 species. It is famous for its bar-less habitats and award-winning exhibits like the Arctic Ring of Life and the Polk Penguin Conservation Center.
attractions.address: 8450 W. 10 Mile Road, Royal Oak, MI 48067
attractions.days_open: Open daily (362 days a year); Closed on Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year’s Day.
highlights: Polk Penguin Conservation Center: The largest penguin facility in the world.
Arctic Ring of Life: One of North America's largest polar bear habitats with an underwater viewing tunnel.
National Amphibian Conservation Center: A premier facility for frog, toad, and salamander conservation.
Australian Outback Adventure: An immersive habitat where kangaroos and wallabies roam freely alongside guests.
The Great Apes of Harambee: A lush 4-acre habitat for chimpanzees and western lowland gorillas.
Cotton Family Wolf Wilderness: A 2-acre sanctuary for gray wolves featuring meadows and a flowing stream.

attractions.name: Eastern Market (Eastern Market)
attractions.description: Eastern Market is the largest historic public market district in the United States, serving as a vibrant community hub for over 130 years. It features a series of open-air sheds where hundreds of vendors sell fresh produce, flowers, meats, and artisanal goods. The surrounding neighborhood is renowned for its eclectic mix of restaurants, local shops, and a massive collection of colorful public murals. (Eastern Market, Wikipedia)
attractions.days_open: Saturdays (Year-round); Tuesdays and Sundays (Seasonal, June–September) (Eastern Market, Visit Detroit)
highlights: Saturday Farmers Market featuring locally grown produce and meats
Flower Day and Flower Tuesday Markets held annually in May
Murals in the Market street art collection throughout the district
Dequindre Cut Greenway pedestrian and bike path connection
Historic Sheds (Sheds 2-6) housing diverse vendors and events
Local culinary staples like Vivio's, Supino Pizzeria, and Germack Coffee (Pure Michigan, Reddit)

attractions.name: Eastern Michigan University (EMU)
attractions.description: Eastern Michigan University (EMU) is a comprehensive public research university founded in 1849 as a teacher-training school. It offers over 200 undergraduate and 150 graduate programs across eight colleges and schools on its 800-acre campus in Ypsilanti. Known for its historical commitment to education and its "Eagles" athletic teams, the university provides a vibrant community with hands-on learning and extensive student resources.
highlights: Historical District featuring Welch, Starkweather, McKenny, and Sherzer Halls listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
The Bruce T. Halle Library, housing a collection of over 1,000,000 items and a state-of-the-art Automated Retrieval System.
Sherzer Observatory, which offers public viewing nights to enjoy views of the moon and stars.
The T.L. Hankinson Vertebrate Museum, home to over 50,000 fish specimens and various other vertebrate collections.
The Student Center, a central hub for student life featuring art galleries and a 24/7 esports facility.
Campus Art Walk, a self-guided tour of 11 sculptures and monuments, including the 9/11 Memorial.

attractions.name: FestiFools (FestiFools Studio)
attractions.description: FestiFools is a one-hour public art spectacular in downtown Ann Arbor featuring giant, community-created papier-mâché puppets. It serves as a celebration of creativity and foolishness, where participants and spectators alike engage with monumental moving art displays. (Destination Ann Arbor, University of Michigan)
attractions.address: Main St (between Washington St and Liberty St), Ann Arbor, MI 48104; Studio at 1239 Kipke Dr, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: Enormous community-made papier-mâché puppets created by U-M students and residents.
Interactive "non-parade" experience where puppets engage directly with the audience.
Themed annual celebrations, such as 2026\
Back to the FOOLture!
The Buckettes
Fool U

attractions.name: Ford Rouge Factory Tour (Ford River Rouge Complex)
attractions.description: The Ford Rouge Factory Tour is an immersive experience located within Ford Motor Company's Dearborn Truck Plant that showcases the past, present, and future of American manufacturing. Visitors can watch the assembly of the Ford F-150 from an elevated walkway and explore sustainable innovation through multisensory theaters and a massive living roof The Henry Ford.
highlights: Legacy Theater: A film presentation detailing the history of the Rouge Complex and Henry Ford's vision.
Manufacturing Innovation Theater: A multisensory experience showcasing how the Ford F-150 is built using advanced technology.
Observation Deck: A panoramic view of the 450,000-square-foot 'Living Roof,' one of the world's largest.
Assembly Plant Walkway: An elevated view over the active F-150 assembly line (subject to production schedules).
Legacy Gallery: A display featuring iconic vehicles manufactured at the Rouge, including the Model A and original Mustang.

attractions.name: Fourth Avenue (Ann Arbor)
attractions.description: Fourth Avenue is a historic and vibrant corridor in downtown Ann Arbor, known for its eclectic mix of shops, restaurants, and transit hubs. It encompasses the historic Ann Street Black Business District and is a central part of the trendy Kerrytown neighborhood. The area is currently undergoing significant streetscape improvements to enhance pedestrian and transit amenities.
attractions.address: Fourth Avenue (North and South), Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: Ann Street Black Business District (historic cultural hub)

attractions.name: Frita Batidos
attractions.description: Frita Batidos is a vibrant Cuban-inspired eatery in downtown Ann Arbor, specializing in two traditional culinary staples: the Frita (a Cuban burger topped with shoestring fries) and Batidos (tropical milkshakes). Founded by Chef Eve Aronoff-Fernandez, the restaurant combines casual picnic-table seating with high-quality, locally sourced ingredients to create a unique community atmosphere. (Frita Batidos Official Site, Pure Michigan)

attractions.category: Park, Outdoor Pool, and Recreation Area
attractions.description: Fuller Park is a 60-acre recreation area featuring extensive athletic fields, a major outdoor pool facility, and one of the city's largest playgrounds. attractions.name: Gallup Park
attractions.description: Gallup Park is a scenic 69-acre recreational area located along the Huron River and Geddes Pond. It is renowned as Ann Arbor's most popular park, featuring island-hopping walkways connected by pedestrian bridges and over three miles of paved trails for biking and running City of Ann Arbor.

attractions.name: Greenfield Village (at The Henry Ford)
attractions.description: Greenfield Village is a premier 80-acre open-air museum that brings three centuries of American life and innovation to life. Created by Henry Ford, the village features over 80 authentic historic structures, including Thomas Edison's Menlo Park laboratory and the Wright brothers' bicycle shop, offering immersive experiences through costumed interpreters and historic transportation. The Henry Ford
attractions.days_open: Seasonal: Daily from mid-May to early September; Tue-Sun in Spring and late September; Fri-Sun in October and November. The Henry Ford

attractions.name: Grizzly Peak Brewing Company
attractions.description: Grizzly Peak Brewing Company has been a staple of downtown Ann Arbor since 1995, offering house-brewed craft beers and a seasonal American menu (Grizzly Peak Brewing Company). Housed in a landmark century-old building, it provides a warm and casual atmosphere with several distinct dining areas and a dedicated basement bar called the Den (Grizzly Peak Brewing Company).

attractions.description: The Heidelberg Restaurant, established in 1961, is a multi-level venue offering a unique German-inspired dining and entertainment experience. It features three distinct areas: the 'Alpine Dining Room' beer hall, the 'Club Above' nightclub, and the 'Rathskeller' basement dive bar (Heidelberg Restaurant).

attractions.name: Henry Ford Museum of American Innovation
attractions.description: The Henry Ford Museum of American Innovation is a premier history destination that immerses visitors in the stories of ingenuity and resourcefulness that shaped the modern world. Its vast collection features landmark artifacts ranging from the Rosa Parks bus to presidential limousines, illustrating 300 years of American innovation. The Henry Ford

attractions.name: Hill Auditorium
attractions.description: Hill Auditorium is a world-renowned concert hall located on the University of Michigan's Central Campus. Designed by the architectural firm Kahn and Wilby and opened in 1913, it is celebrated for its exceptional acoustical design and unique parabolic ceiling. As the largest performance space on campus, it has hosted a legendary array of artists, from Leonard Bernstein and Cecilia Bartoli to Bob Marley and Bob Dylan. University of Michigan SMTD, Wikipedia

It offers an extensive selection of draft beers, award-winning food like their famous Cosmik Fries, and an intimate atmosphere with a small sidewalk patio in the heart of downtown.

attractions.name: Huron River (Huron River National Water Trail)
attractions.description: The Huron River is a 130-mile-long river in southeastern Michigan that flows through the heart of Ann Arbor, providing extensive recreational opportunities. Designated as a National Water Trail, it features the popular Argo Cascades for tubing and several scenic parks for kayaking, fishing, and hiking. The river is a central part of the community's outdoor life, supported by the Huron River Watershed Council and managed liveries at Argo and Gallup Parks. Huron River Water Trail, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily (Gallup Park and Livery during season); Weekends (Argo Livery during May). City of Ann Arbor

attractions.name: James Burrill Angell Hall
attractions.description: Designed by renowned architect Albert Kahn and completed in 1924, James Burrill Angell Hall is a classical Greek-style academic building serving as the centerpiece of the University of Michigan's College of Literature, Science, and the Arts. It is famously fronted by eight massive Doric columns and houses numerous classrooms, administrative offices, and specialized facilities like a rooftop observatory and a major computing center.
highlights: Eight massive Doric columns and classical entablature on the State Street facade.
Angell Hall Observatory on the fifth-floor roof, featuring a 16-inch reflecting telescope.
The 'Fishbowl,' a prominent and high-traffic student computing center and study space.
The Northwest Ordinance of 1787 inscription carved across the front facade.
Historic lobby finished in elegant travertine marble with intricate mythological carvings.

Known for traditional Belgian brewing methods like open fermentation and barrel-aging, the location provides an adventurous dining experience in a rustic, whimsical downtown setting (Follow The Piper, Jolly Pumpkin Brewery).

attractions.name: Kerrytown District
attractions.description: Kerrytown is a historic neighborhood and retail destination in Ann Arbor, Michigan, known for its charming brick streets, 19th-century buildings, and vibrant local culture. The district features a diverse array of specialty shops, restaurants, and the iconic Ann Arbor Farmers Market, offering a unique blend of heritage and community-focused commerce (Kerrytown Market & Shops, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Liberty Street (Ann Arbor)
attractions.description: Liberty Street is a vibrant commercial and cultural artery in downtown Ann Arbor, connecting the historic West Side to the University of Michigan campus. It is renowned for its diverse mix of independent boutiques, award-winning restaurants, and iconic landmarks like the Michigan Theater. During summer and fall weekends, segments of the street often transform into a pedestrian-only zone featuring outdoor dining and live performances.

attractions.name: Main Street Ann Arbor
attractions.description: Main Street Ann Arbor is the vibrant heart of downtown Ann Arbor, renowned for its eclectic mix of award-winning restaurants, unique boutiques, and historic charm. The area serves as a central hub for community life, hosting major annual events and offering a walkable environment that blends academic energy with urban sophistication. It is managed and promoted by the Main Street Area Association to ensure its continued vitality and success.

attractions.name: Michigan League
attractions.description: The Michigan League is a historic student union at the University of Michigan, featuring stunning Collegiate Gothic architecture and stained-glass windows. Opened in 1929, it provides a hub for campus life with dining at Maizie’s Kitchen & Market, elegant event spaces, and the 21-room Inn at the League. It also houses the Lydia Mendelssohn Theatre and is bordered by a beautiful courtyard garden and the iconic 'Sunday Morning in Deep Waters' fountain.

attractions.name: Michigan Stadium (The Big House)
attractions.description: Michigan Stadium, famously known as "The Big House," is the iconic home of the University of Michigan Wolverines football team in Ann Arbor. As the largest stadium in the United States and the third-largest in the world, it boasts an official seating capacity of 107,601 but frequently hosts crowds exceeding 115,000. Originally opened in 1927, the venue is a cornerstone of American college football history and offers guided tours that provide behind-the-scenes access to the field and locker rooms.

attractions.name: Michigan Theater
attractions.description: The Michigan Theater is a premier historic movie palace and cultural hub in downtown Ann Arbor, originally opened in 1928 and designed by architect Maurice Finkel. It features a fully operational stage, an orchestra pit, and a rare, fully restored 1928 Barton Theatre Pipe Organ. Today, it serves as a non-profit venue for independent films, live concerts, stage productions, and community events. (Marquee Arts), (Wikipedia))

attractions.name: Scorekeepers Bar & Grill
attractions.description: Known affectionately as "Skeeps," Scorekeepers is an iconic Ann Arbor sports bar and institution that has served University of Michigan students and locals since 1974. It offers a vibrant atmosphere for game days with over 35 TVs, transitioning into a late-night destination featuring live DJs and a huge dance floor. Official Website
highlights: Over 35 televisions for broadcasting a wide variety of sporting events.
Expansive selection of 37 different beers on tap and in bottles.
Famous menu items including cheeseburgers, nachos, and wings.
Large dance floor with live DJs playing club and throwback music.
Daily happy hour specials and themed nights like "Thirsty Thursday."
Centrally located in the heart of downtown Ann Arbor's student life area.

attractions.name: Seva Restaurant
attractions.description: Seva Restaurant has been a cornerstone of the Ann Arbor dining scene since 1973, offering fresh and imaginative vegetarian cuisine. Originally established in Columbus, Ohio in 1971, the restaurant moved to its current home in the Westgate Shopping Center in 2014, where it continues to serve a diverse menu of vegan and gluten-free friendly dishes. The space is known for its warm, inclusive atmosphere, featuring artful stained glass, lush plants, and vibrant rainbow decor.
highlights: Fresh, imaginative vegetarian and vegan cuisine since 1973.
Extensive menu featuring global flavors, from Mexican entrees to Thai-inspired stir-fries.
Full bar offering cocktails, smoothies, fresh juices, and a selection of micro-beers.
Family-friendly environment with a dedicated children's menu and ample parking.
Celebrated history documented in their 50th Anniversary Cookbook.
Commitment to an inclusive, LGBTQ+ friendly atmosphere with peace and hope symbols.

attractions.name: Sindecuse Museum of Dentistry
attractions.description: The Sindecuse Museum of Dentistry is one of a handful of museums in the world devoted to preserving and exhibiting the history of dentistry. Housed within the University of Michigan School of Dentistry, it features over 25,000 objects dating from the 18th century to the present day, focusing on dental practice and technology. (Official Website)
highlights: Inside the Dental Practice: 1860-2000 exhibit featuring evolved equipment (Official Website)
Teeth Transformations: Modifications for Self-Expression (Official Website)
Dental Hygiene: A Century of Progress (Official Website)
Tooth Fairy Magic: History of the imaginary Tooth Fairy (Official Website)
Collection of over 25,000 historical dental artifacts (Official Website)
Student Reflections: A Retrospective of Dental Education (Official Website)

attractions.name: State Street District
attractions.description: The State Street District is a vibrant urban core and nonprofit membership association that has served downtown Ann Arbor for over fifty years. It acts as a 24/7 engine for the city's vitality, fostering a thriving and inclusive community through strategic partnerships, events, and initiatives. The district is renowned for its blend of historic charm, student energy, and world-class cultural institutions.
attractions.address: 527 E. Liberty, Suite 208, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: State Street Art Fair: One of the nation's largest juried art fairs held every July.
Graffiti Alley: A constantly evolving public art space and popular photo destination.
The State Theatre: An iconic Art Deco cinema featuring independent and classic films.
University of Michigan Museum of Art (UMMA): A premier campus museum with extensive collections.
Nickels Arcade: A beautiful historic glass-roofed shopping arcade dating back to 1918.
Hill Auditorium: A world-renowned concert hall known for its perfect acoustics.

attractions.name: The Ark The Ark
attractions.category: Entertainment / Music Venue Destination Ann Arbor
attractions.description: The Ark is Michigan's non-profit home for folk and roots music, considered one of the premier acoustic music clubs in the world. It provides an intimate listening room experience for over 300 live performances annually, featuring a wide range of genres from legendary folk icons to emerging local talent The Ark About, Pure Michigan

attractions.name: The Blind Pig
attractions.description: The Blind Pig is a legendary live music venue and nightclub in Ann Arbor that has been a cultural staple since its opening in 1971. Originally a cafe and blues club, it evolved into a premiere spot for indie, rock, and alternative acts, famously hosting Nirvana before their rise to global stardom. Today, it continues to showcase an eclectic mix of local talent and national touring artists in an intimate setting.
attractions.address: 208 S. First Street, Ann Arbor, MI 48104

attractions.name: The Brown Jug Restaurant
attractions.description: The Brown Jug Restaurant has been an iconic part of Ann Arbor and University of Michigan tradition since 1936. Named after the famous Michigan vs. Minnesota football trophy, it is a beloved campus institution where many menu items are named after well-known Wolverine alumni. The Brown Jug Story

attractions.name: The Diag (University of Michigan)
attractions.description: The Diag is the oldest and most central area of the University of Michigan campus in Ann Arbor. Originally known as the Diagonal Green, it is a large open space famous for its crisscrossing sidewalks and historical significance. It serves as a major hub for student life, protests, and university traditions.
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Center for Campus Involvement)
highlights: Block M: A large brass 'M' set into the center of the Diag; tradition holds that stepping on it will cause a student to fail their first bluebook exam (Michigan Ross)

Student Hub: A popular site for student organizations to host events, fundraisers, and demonstrations (Center for Campus Involvement)
Central Location: Positioned at the heart of Central Campus, connecting major academic and social buildings (Apple Maps)

attractions.name: The Last Word
attractions.description: The Last Word is an intimate, speakeasy-style cocktail and whiskey bar located in downtown Ann Arbor. It features a dimly lit, atmospheric setting with an extensive library of spirits and a menu of expertly crafted cocktails and high-end social plates. (Tripadvisor, YouTube Review)
attractions.address: 301 W Huron St, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: Extensive spirit library with a focus on a diverse whiskey selection (Official Website)
Speakeasy-inspired atmosphere with an unmarked entrance (Tripadvisor)

Interactive cocktail classes and a rotating weekly lineup of events (Official Website)

attractions.name: The Pretzel Bell
attractions.description: The Pretzel Bell originally opened its doors in 1934 and became the campus watering hole of choice for nearly 50 years. Today, it offers updated American comfort food and craft cocktails in a bi-level space filled with University of Michigan memorabilia and historic photographs. It serves as a central gathering place for students, alumni, and locals to celebrate Maize & Blue pride.
attractions.address: 226 S Main St, Ann Arbor, MI 48104
highlights: Historic campus watering hole tradition since 1934.
Extensive collection of University of Michigan sports memorabilia and iconic photographs.
Signature "Down Bar" featuring live music, trivia, and games.
Menu featuring updated American comfort food like the P-Bell Burger and wings.
Famous warm soft-baked pretzels served with ale house cheese and honey mustard.
Central location in the heart of the downtown Ann Arbor shopping district.

attractions.name: The Purple Rose Theatre Company
attractions.description: Founded in 1991 by actor Jeff Daniels, The Purple Rose Theatre Company is a premier regional theater in Chelsea, Michigan, dedicated to producing world-class original American plays. It focuses on showcasing Midwestern voices and provides a professional home for both emerging talent and seasoned artists. The Purple Rose Theatre Company

attractions.name: Top of the Park (Ann Arbor Summer Festival)
attractions.description: Top of the Park is the admission-free outdoor centerpiece of the Ann Arbor Summer Festival (A2SF), held annually on the University of Michigan campus. This vibrant community tradition features a diverse lineup of live music, outdoor movie screenings, family activities, and local culinary offerings in a festive, open-air setting (A2SF, Destination Ann Arbor).
highlights: Admission-free live concerts featuring local and national artists on the Rackham and O&W Grove stages (A2SF)
"Movies by Moonlight" outdoor film screenings under the stars (A2SF)
KidZone offering hands-on activities and science discovery for children ages 2-10 (A2SF)
Culinary Row showcasing a variety of local food and beverage vendors (A2SF)

attractions.name: University of Michigan (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The University of Michigan is a preeminent public research institution founded in 1817, recognized for its rigorous academics and vibrant campus life. Its expansive Ann Arbor campus serves as a cultural and intellectual hub, featuring world-class museums, historic architecture, and the iconic Michigan Stadium. (Wikipedia, University of Michigan)
attractions.address: 500 S State St, Ann Arbor, MI 48109 (University of Michigan)

Law Quadrangle: A stunning example of Collegiate Gothic architecture (Wikipedia)
Michigan Stadium (The Big House): The largest stadium in the United States (Wikipedia)

Nichols Arboretum: A scenic 123-acre nature preserve perfect for walking and relaxation (SheBuysTravel)
Burton Memorial Tower: An iconic campus landmark housing a 55-bell grand carillon (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: University of Michigan Law Quadrangle University of Michigan Law School
attractions.category: University, Landmark, Architectural Site Destination Ann Arbor
attractions.description: The University of Michigan Law Quadrangle is an iconic architectural complex built between 1924 and 1933, featuring stunning Collegiate Gothic buildings inspired by the universities of Oxford and Cambridge. It serves as a self-contained home for the Law School, housing academic facilities, a world-class law library, and residential space for students within a serene, enclosed courtyard. University of Michigan Law School, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 625 S State St, Ann Arbor, MI 48109 Destination Ann Arbor

attractions.name: University of Michigan Matthaei Botanical Gardens
attractions.description: Matthaei Botanical Gardens is a 300-acre site operated by the University of Michigan that serves as a living museum for environmental education and research. The facility features eleven distinct outdoor gardens, an expansive indoor multi-climate conservatory, and miles of scenic nature trails. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1800 N Dixboro Rd, Ann Arbor, MI 48105
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Conservatory and Visitor Center); Daily (Outdoor gardens and trails) Official Website

attractions.name: University of Michigan Museum of Art (UMMA)
attractions.description: The University of Michigan Museum of Art (UMMA) is one of the largest university art museums in the United States, housing a comprehensive collection of over 20,000 objects. It serves as a meeting place for the arts, bridging visual art and contemporary culture through diverse exhibitions and educational programs. The museum's historic Alumni Memorial Hall and modern Frankel Family Wing provide a dynamic setting for exploring art spanning cultures and eras. (Wikipedia, UMMA)
attractions.address: 525 S State St, Ann Arbor, MI 48109
highlights: A diverse collection spanning 150 years with over 20,000 objects from various cultures and eras. (UMMA)
Renowned works by artists such as James McNeill Whistler and Pablo Picasso. (ClickOnDetroit)
Extensive Asian art collection, including Chinese and Japanese paintings and ceramics. (UMMA)
The historic Alumni Memorial Hall, originally built in 1909 as a war memorial. (Wikipedia)
The modern Maxine and Stuart Frankel and the Frankel Family Wing expansion. (UMMA)
Strong collections of African art from the 19th and 20th centuries. (ClickOnDetroit)

attractions.name: University of Michigan Museum of Natural History
attractions.description: The University of Michigan Museum of Natural History tells the story of our world through interactive exhibits and hands-on experiences. It showcases the university's research collections, including extensive displays on evolution, prehistoric life, and Michigan's natural history (Official Site).
attractions.address: Biological Sciences Building, 1105 North University Avenue, Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1085

On the Trail of the Mastodons (male and female skeletons)
Planetarium & Dome Theater
Fossil Prep Lab & Biodiversity Lab (Science at Work)

attractions.name: Veterans Memorial Park
attractions.description: Veterans Memorial Park is a premier 37-acre recreational hub in Ann Arbor that serves as one of the city's busiest community spaces. The park features a diverse array of amenities including a large outdoor pool complex, an indoor ice arena, a world-class skatepark, and extensive sports fields. It is named in honor of U.S. military veterans, with a dedicated memorial located on-site. City of Ann Arbor

attractions.name: Vinology Restaurant & Event Space
attractions.description: Vinology is a sophisticated downtown venue that focuses on creative, wine-friendly world cuisine and extensive pairings curated by a James Beard Award-winning executive chef. Set within the historic Mayer Schairer Office Supply Building, the restaurant features unique architectural details and intimate dining areas designed for both casual visits and grand celebrations. It is particularly renowned for its enchanting "Bubble Room," which is illuminated by hundreds of hand-blown glass balls.
attractions.address: 110 S Main St, Ann Arbor, MI 48104

attractions.name: Waterloo State Recreation Area
attractions.description: Waterloo Recreation Area is the third-largest state park in Michigan and the largest in the Lower Peninsula, encompassing over 21,000 acres of forest, lakes, and wetlands. It offers a diverse array of outdoor activities including camping, fishing, hunting, and over 50 miles of trails for hiking, mountain biking, and horseback riding. Wikipedia
highlights: Largest state park in the Lower Peninsula with over 21,000 acres. Wikipedia
Home to 11 inland lakes providing excellent fishing, swimming, and boating. Michigan DNR

The DTE Energy Foundation Trail offers 20+ miles of premier mountain biking terrain. Wikipedia

attractions.name: West Park
attractions.description: West Park is a sprawling 27-acre recreational space located just west of downtown Ann Arbor, featuring rolling grassy hills and majestic historic trees. The park is home to unique environmental features like bioswales and a pond, as well as a historic band shell that has long served as a community hub for music and theater performances. (City of Ann Arbor, Ann Arbor Observer)

attractions.name: Ypsilanti Historic District
attractions.description: The Ypsilanti Historic District is one of Michigan's largest contiguous historic areas, featuring over 200 buildings that showcase a diverse array of 19th and early 20th-century architectural styles. Centered around the Huron River, the district includes the city's vibrant downtown commercial core, the historic Depot Town area, and several beautifully preserved residential neighborhoods.

attractions.name: Ypsilanti Water Tower
attractions.description: The Ypsilanti Water Tower is a historic 147-foot-tall structure built in 1890 using Joliet limestone. Located at the city's highest point, it is a prominent landmark known for its unique Queen Anne-style architecture and the marble bust of Demetrios Ypsilantis at its base.

attractions.name: Zingerman's Bakehouse
attractions.description: Zingerman's Bakehouse is an artisanal bakery that opened in 1992 to provide high-quality bread for Zingerman's Delicatessen. It has since evolved into a full-service bakery and teaching school, specializing in traditional American, European, and Jewish recipes made with many locally sourced and stone-milled grains. The site features a bustling retail shop, a cake studio for custom-designed cakes, and an onsite teaching bakery for home bakers.
highlights: Artisanal breads made with traditional recipes and stone-milled regional grains.
BAKE!, an onsite teaching bakery offering hands-on baking and cooking classes.

attractions.name: Zingerman's Delicatessen Zingerman's Deli
attractions.description: Opened in 1982 by Paul Saginaw and Ari Weinzweig, Zingerman's Delicatessen is a world-renowned Ann Arbor institution famous for its massive, high-quality sandwiches and extensive selection of specialty foods. Located in a historic 1902 brick building, it offers everything from farmhouse cheeses and estate-bottled olive oils to housemade chopped liver and its legendary Reuben. Zingerman's Deli, Wikipedia
